channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Huge Hole, 17th April
item.description:
This pothole has deteriorated significantly and is now a serious hazard to road users. It is located at a busy junction/roundabout where vehicles are required to slow, turn and merge, which increases the likelihood of impact. Due to its depth, width and position in the carriageway, it presents a clear risk of damage to tyres, wheels, suspension and steering components. The defect also creates a safety risk for drivers, cyclists and motorcyclists, particularly in poor weather or low visibility when it may be harder to see. There is another similar defect nearby, which suggests the road surface in this area is failing and requires urgent inspection. This issue should be treated as a priority and repaired immediately before it causes vehicle damage or leads to an accident.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elm Street, Wymans Brook, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large potholes causing damage to vehicles, 16th April
item.description:
End of broadclose road at the main road junction
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Broadclose Road, Down Hatherley, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road surface broken up and larger holes forming, 16th April
item.description:
The junction turning from the A40 onto Telstar Way has has a poor surface since the A40 was resurfaced. It’s now getting much worse and turning into and out of Telstar Way is dangerous as cars swerve to avoid the holes.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Telstar Way, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A number of potholes have now joined together, 16th April
item.description:
Outside the side door of the greyhound pub in the main road. a number of potholes have joined together to form one huge pothole. I now try and drive on the other side of the road to avoid it but sometimes end up facing oncoming traffic.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Greyhound Gardens, Elmbridge, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 16th April
item.description:
Permanent Repair CWPH 1100 x 600 x 97mm Marked as X Road Closure (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 16th April
item.description:
ENWD CWPH 1100 x 600 x 97mm Marked as X Road Closure (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -other CW6 problem, 16th April
item.description:
CWED 900 x 400 x 68mm Road Closure (CW6 Edge Deterioration)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road surface crumbled & a chunk out, 16th April
item.description:
Going towards Prescott Hill from Gotherington there is a section thatslopes to the left. The surface has broken away & a is now there
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Prescott, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Numerous Potholes, 16th April
item.description:
As previously reported these potholes in Salamanca Road, back last year and your email on 5th Dec 25 you would look into it, nothing done so I reported again as you will see from the records, your workers have driven up the road this morning and sprayed paint around all the potholes on the lower half of the street but from the corner to no 30 not one drop of white paint and they are as bad as the ones lower down can you explain why, is it because it us higher nujmbers that have reported it and you are making us suffer even more and just attending to the lower house numbers problems an explanation please would be great
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Kimberley Walk, Oakley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
